meta.title: DCM Weekly : দেশী Macro
meta.subtitle: Rates, FX, the External Sector & Global Markets
meta.forAudience: For Quick Reference Only - Domestic & Global Markets
meta.headline: Bangladesh Bank maintains its contractionary policy rate at 10.0% through H1FY27 to anchor inflation—now easing to 9.4% from an 11.7% peak—while unveiling a Tk. 60,000 crore stimulus package aimed at reviving private credit, industrial output, and generating 25 lakh jobs; even as the economy shows a fragile recovery with GDP growth targeted at 6.5%, elevated NPLs near 32%, stable reserves and remittances, sweeping banking-sector reforms, and mounting external risks from Middle East conflict-driven energy and fertilizer price shocks.

commentary.rates: The Bangladesh Bank has lowered its private sector credit growth target to 6.8% for December under its Monetary Policy Statement for the first half of FY2026-27.
commentary.ycurve: Bangladesh Bank (BB) has capped the maximum Merchant Discount Rate (MDR) at one percent, including VAT, for government revenue and fee collection through the Bangla QR payment system to promote affordable and wider use of digital payments.
commentary.inflation: The Bangladesh Bank’s (BB) own inflation model is running well ahead of its own expectations, projecting price growth near 9 percent through mid-2027, even as its official ceiling sits at 7.5 percent for the upcoming fiscal year 2026-27, according to BRAC EPL Stock Brokerage.
commentary.external: According to the latest data with Bangladesh Bank (BB), with June's provisional count of $2.80 billion the total remittance netting in FY26 came to $35.56 billion, $5.23-billion higher from the FY'25 receipt.

fx.text: The taka rose 0.01 to 122.85/USD, with BC selling at 123.75.

global.text: Term SOFR spans 3.75-4.03% across tenors, while EURIBOR sits lower at 2.33%+.
The 3-month SOFR-EURIBOR gap is 1.42pp, a live input for currency-of-borrowing decisions.

rates.text: The Bangladesh Bank has lowered its private sector credit growth target to 6.8% for December under its Monetary Policy Statement for the first half of FY2026-27.
The repo rate is 10.00%, the Bank Rate 4.00%.

ycurve.text: Bangladesh Bank (BB) has capped the maximum Merchant Discount Rate (MDR) at one percent, including VAT, for government revenue and fee collection through the Bangla QR payment system to promote affordable and wider use of digital payments.
The local-currency curve is upward-sloping: the 91-day (9.44%) sits below the 10-year (10.24%).
Week-on-week the curve shifted down about 0.09pp on average.

inflation.text: The Bangladesh Bank’s (BB) own inflation model is running well ahead of its own expectations, projecting price growth near 9 percent through mid-2027, even as its official ceiling sits at 7.5 percent for the upcoming fiscal year 2026-27, according to BRAC EPL Stock Brokerage.

external.text: According to the latest data with Bangladesh Bank (BB), with June's provisional count of $2.80 billion the total remittance netting in FY26 came to $35.56 billion, $5.23-billion higher from the FY'25 receipt.
Exports were $4.20bn against imports of $5.80bn - a trade gap of $1.60bn.

reserves.text: Gross reserves was little changed at $36.08bn (BPM6 $31.53bn).

calendar.event: BBS - June & FY26 CPI inflation (point-to-point and 12-month average)
Bangladesh Bank - month-end gross & BPM6 reserves; FY26 remittance total
EPB - June & FY26 export earnings and the trade balance
Bangladesh Bank - T-bill & T-bond auction cut-off yields
Bangladesh Bank - Monetary Policy Statement for H1 FY27
Global - term SOFR, EURIBOR, gold and Brent crude

special.title: Bangladesh Bank Monetary Policy Statement (H1 FY27) — Summary
points: Policy rate held at 10.0% (unchanged since Oct 2024); SLF at 11.5%, SDF at 7.5% — contractionary stance continues into H1FY27
Inflation: eased from 11.7% (Jul 2024) to 9.4% (May 2026); projected to moderate to ~8.9% by Dec 2026
Growth: FY26 GDP growth ~4.14%; FY27 target 6.5% (BB forecasts 6.1%)
Tk. 60,000 crore stimulus package for industry, agriculture & CMSMEs — expected to create ~25 lakh jobs
Private sector credit growth slowed to 5.5% (Jun 2026); projected to recover to 8.0% by Jun 2027
NPLs remain elevated: gross NPL ratio at 32.26% (Mar 2026); new Bank Resolution Act & Deposit Protection Act 2026 introduced
External sector strengthened: reserves rose to $31.74B; remittances up 19.07%; exchange rate stable (~BDT 123/USD)
Key risks: Middle East conflict, energy/fertilizer price shocks, high NPLs, weak private investment
